text: If there's ever a problem in an
organization or department or a line of
revenue, look at the leader. And here's
the here's the rule. The leader is
always the problem. Not sometimes. The
leader is always the problem.
So, if a function's mediocre, it's
because the leader is mediocre, period.
Full stop. No questions. So, it's either
the leader and the team or just the
leader, but it's never just the team.
So, if the leader is mediocre and then
they brought in mediocre people, it's
the leader and the team. If the leader
is mediocre and you've got a great team,
the leader will turn the great team into
a mediocre team. And so, I had a a
conversation with a business owner the
other day and he had four locations. Two
of them were doing well, two of them
were doing poorly. I asked a zillion
questions to finally get to the the the
the the fun fact, which is that he had
somebody in the company who was running

also, we'll just say an addict. I'll
just say that. And every day he was
coming up to work
messed up, inebriated, whatever you want
to say. And he was asking me for
tactical business advice when I was
like, "There is no tactical business
advice. There is nothing that will
matter because every No one will listen

like all these questions like pricing
